post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 150!
Credits to Binomial-theorem/chaotic_iak/willwang123 (among others) for the rule template.

Story
haha no


Rules
  • Read the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Do not post at night.
  • Do not post in this game unless you are one of the players listed.
  • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
  • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs. Do not play around with exploiting this rule.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderator in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.


Voting/Lynches
  • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: fdas" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", "vote: fdas", "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: fdsa", "Vote: Fdas" and "Vote: fdas" are not.
  • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
  • Improperly made votes (including votes in hide tags or any of the unacceptable forms above) will not be counted under any circumstances. I will not tell you if your vote is invalid.
  • You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Lynch".
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
  • When the deadline is reached, the player with the most votes will be lynched. If there is a tie, one player will be chosen at random.
  • Starting Day 2, If a majority of players are voting for a single player, that player will be lynched, regardless of when the deadline is.
  • On any day, if at least half of the players are voting for No Lynch, no player will be lynched, and it will proceed to twilight
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
  • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
  • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.


Activity
  • Each day will be 168 hours (7 days). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur the very second 168 hours have happened.
  • If the deadline is met and no person is lynched, it will be considered no lynch and will proceed to be twilight.
  • Each night will be 3 real-life days long.
  • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive an action from a player, that player will not perform an action. If the moderator does not receive a kill from any member of the mafia faction, a random member of that faction will attempt to killa random non-mafia player.
  • Any night results will be sent after the night deadline.
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day.
  • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 36-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
  • If a players has been at replacement status for more than 5 days when it changes from night to day or vice versa, they will be modkilled.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than about 5 days, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.
  • I don't expect you to read all of the posts in this thread if there are a lot of them. I expect that none of the other players will expect this of you either.
  • You may not post more than 75 times in a single day phase. If you continue to post after you are told to stop, you will be modkilled.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • If you have committed several rule infractions (“several” is loosely defined, but is smaller for more experienced players) or one major rule infraction, you will be modkilled or force-replaced. If you are modkilled, regardless of whether or not your faction wins, you will still be declared a loser.
  • A modkill will not end the day; a normal lynch will still occur.


Moderators
  • I (whatshisbucket) am the Moderator of Game 150. summitwei and jonyj1005 are the comoderators of Game 150.
  • Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
  • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
  • Green-colored text (#38761D) is used for town flavor-related posts.
  • To show that you have read the rules, send in your Role PM a guess for the number of players that will be alive at the start of Day 2. The closest player(s) still alive at that point will receive a prize and the farthest player will be killed. If there is a tie for the farthest player, one will be killed at random. Do not discuss this in the game thread. You may change your guess up until 4 days from the start of Day 1. Doing so will result in a warning. This counts as one of the three events. After 4 days have passed, those who have confirmed will no longer be able to change their guess. Those who have not confirmed at that point may still do so until the end of Day 1, but they will be ineligible for nay prizes, and can still die if their guess is the worst.
  • Please do not post in those colors or in any shades of those colors.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.


Setup
Closed. There are some PRs, a town and mafia faction, and at least one 3rd party player. Who got what role was randomized with random.org.
Do not flavorclaim. It is strictly forbidden. If you do so, you will be modkilled.
You also are strongly discouraged from roleclaiming. Since some of the roles are unique, it is very difficult to counterclaim. If you sofclaim that you are a Town PR, the mafia can permanently roleblock you if they provide evidence that you softclaimed. If you hardclaim a non-vanilla town role, the mafia can kill you the subsequent night, in addition to their regular kill. You can be roleblocked or killed as punishment even if you lied about your role.


Special Mechanics
Your flavor name will be another current or former AoPS Mafia! Player.
There will be three events during the game, each of which will result in the player who does the most poorly being killed and one or more players receiving a prize.


Role PMs
Not provided as this is a closed setup, but here is an example VT role PM that is not being used:
VT role PM wrote:
Hello [Player], you are theoneforce, or Vanilla Townie. Old player, modder of my personal favorite game, and creator of a Mafia Bot which is completely unused now, it’s only right we bring you back for this game as a VT. Good luck.

post_rendered:
dialation14551 wrote:
wait what is the game

It's a game called Mafia.

Here's a quote from mafiascum.net:
Quote:
In its simplest form, Mafia consists of two sides: the Mafia, and the Town. The Mafia's goal is to kill the innocents, while the Town's goal is to kill the Mafia.

At the beginning of the game, the Moderator will secretly send everyone their role. The Mafia will know each other, while (in the basic game) everyone else will only know their own role.

The game has two phases, the first of which is Day. During Day, the Town must choose someone to Lynch; they are trying to eliminate the Mafia, but the Mafia can lead them astray by casting suspicion elsewhere. Generally, players will vote for someone they think is Mafia (the Mafia will vote so that they seem to be doing this as well, and might even vote for each other to confuse the Town); when a player gets a majority of the votes, they are lynched. Their role is revealed by the Moderator, and it becomes Night.

During the Night, the thread is locked and no one can talk publicly. The Mafia, however, can speak to each other privately to plot strategy and decide who to murder before the next Day begins. In this way, they can silence members of the Town who are threats to them. At the end of the Night, the murder is carried out and another Day begins. In effect, the Town gets to kill someone, and then the Mafia gets to kill someone.

The game alternates between Day and Night until either the Mafia are eliminated, or the Mafia have killed everyone else (or nothing can prevent the same).

This post will be deleted upon request or the start of Day 1.

Edit: Neither of you post, please. Do not respond on this thread; instead, PM me or whatshisbucket if you want to learn more.
post_canonical:
[quote=dialation14551]wait what is the game[/quote]

It's a game called Mafia.

Here's a quote from mafiascum.net:

[quote]In its simplest form, Mafia consists of two sides: the Mafia, and the Town. The Mafia's goal is to kill the innocents, while the Town's goal is to kill the Mafia.

At the beginning of the game, the Moderator will secretly send everyone their role. The Mafia will know each other, while (in the basic game) everyone else will only know their own role.

The game has two phases, the first of which is Day. During Day, the Town must choose someone to Lynch; they are trying to eliminate the Mafia, but the Mafia can lead them astray by casting suspicion elsewhere. Generally, players will vote for someone they think is Mafia (the Mafia will vote so that they seem to be doing this as well, and might even vote for each other to confuse the Town); when a player gets a majority of the votes, they are lynched. Their role is revealed by the Moderator, and it becomes Night.

During the Night, the thread is locked and no one can talk publicly. The Mafia, however, can speak to each other privately to plot strategy and decide who to murder before the next Day begins. In this way, they can silence members of the Town who are threats to them. At the end of the Night, the murder is carried out and another Day begins. In effect, the Town gets to kill someone, and then the Mafia gets to kill someone.

The game alternates between Day and Night until either the Mafia are eliminated, or the Mafia have killed everyone else (or nothing can prevent the same). [/quote]

This post will be deleted upon request or the start of Day 1.

Edit: Neither of you post, please. Do not respond on this thread; instead, [b]PM me or whatshisbucket[/b] if you want to learn more.

"Closed. There are some PRs, a town and mafia faction, and at least one 3rd party player. Who got what role was randomized with random.org.
Do not flavorclaim. It is strictly forbidden. If you do so, you will be modkilled.
You also are strongly discouraged from roleclaiming. Since some of the roles are unique, it is very difficult to counterclaim. "

but town killing roles cannot claim. So we essentially kill one if we mislynch, and if any one town PR dies, we are in a worse position then not lynching at all.
Yes, your logic works but only if they can convince us they are a killing role, but this is difficult.

My opinion on a 32 ppl game of mafia with 1 game day being 1 game week is that it's way too complex, and discussion on the first day is terribly hard to follow. Actions on the following days could tell us more, but without the ability to even role claim, even that may not be possible. I rather dislike this already.

Event
Blotto!
You are Colonel Blotto, and you are at war. You have 100 battalions of soldiers that you want to divide between nine key locations where battles will take place. You can divide up the battalions in integer amounts and distribute them between the ten locations. At each location, a battle take place, and each will have a different weight in determining who wins the war between you and your enemy. The weights are 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19, 23.

Your submission should be an ordered sequence of nine integers. The first number in the sequence will represent the number of battalions you are sending to the location with weight 2, the second number will represent the number of battalions you are sending to the location with weight 3, and so on. Every two submissions will be compared to determine who wins the war. For example, consider the submissions 11,11,11,11,11,11,11,11,12 and 10,10,10,10,10,10,10,11,19. The first submission would win battles at the locations with weights 2,3,5,7,11,13, and 17, for a total of 58 points. The second submission would receive 23 points for winning only that location. No one would receive any points for the location where the armies where of equal size.

Each submission will be compared against the submissions of the other 8 players. Your score will be an integer from 0 to 8, representing the number of wars you won. In case of a tie, the tiebreaker will be the total weight of all battles won between the 8 wars. The winner will be the player with the highest score, and the loser will be the player with the lowest score. You have 36 hours to send your submissions in your Role PM. Good luck!

Now I'm going to give my case for No Lynch today. Again. Because people are dismissing it since they say "this is just another MG, we should also lynch". That's being really lazy and not bothering to understand this game well.
My case for a no lynch
Yes, a lynch on D1 is useful in most other mafia games. Agreed. But let's look at this game again and understand the differences from other games that have been played.
1. There are 32 people and closed setup.
2. It's one week long. [Just had to put it out there]
3. Hard roleclaiming can get you killed.

So this game is very different from basically every other MG in aops. And so the theory can vary a little. I believe one of these is whether we lynch day 1.

First we must first examine the arguments that support a D1 lynch.
1. It allows for a MYLO instead of LYLO if we need it.
2. It gives town the power to kill who they want.
3. It get rids of a worse townie, making town better off, even in the worst case.


So why do these reasons not work here?
1. This isn't a Newbie game, we don't know how many die per week and more.
2. We may accidentally lynch a town killing role, and in this game they can't roleclaim against it.
3. I strongly believe a lynch in one week won't be good. Reading so many messages a day with so many people and extremely complex relationships is difficult and almost unmanageable. In fact, some people haven't even confirmed yet! Scum can more easily get a lynch on someone who is actually good and given there are so many people, bandwagoning is easier and harder to track. tl;dr it's easier for scum to get away with killing a good townie then other games.

DISCLAIMER: I am not against scumhunting. Have your suspicions all you want; they will help later on and can have impacts later on in the game. Ways this can happen is: 1. You influence a town killing role to actually kill scum/3rd. 2. You influence votes later.
So scumhunting can continue!

1. We do not go with no information; we have the night kills.
2. I have explained why no lynch shouldn't be done, and I guess I'll have to do it again.
3. NL is bad on Newbie games and C9 and the rest because of the parity, and partially also because [b]people can roleclaim without dying.[/b].
[/hide]

Now I'm going to give my case for No Lynch today. Again. Because people are dismissing it since they say "this is just another MG, we should also lynch". That's being really lazy and not bothering to understand this game well. 
[hide=My case for a no lynch]
Yes, a lynch on D1 is useful in most other mafia games. Agreed. But let's look at this game again and understand the differences from other games that have been played.
1. There are 32 people and closed setup.
2. It's one week long. [Just had to put it out there]
3. [b]Hard roleclaiming can get you killed.[/b]

So this game is very different from basically every other MG in aops. And so the theory can vary a little. I believe one of these is whether we lynch day 1. 

First we must first examine the arguments that support a D1 lynch.
[b]1. It allows for a MYLO instead of LYLO if we need it. [/b]
[b]2. It gives town the power to kill who they want.[/b]
[b]3. It get rids of a worse townie, making town better off, even in the worst case.[/b]


So why do these reasons not work here?
[b]1. This isn't a Newbie game, we don't know how many die per week and more.[/b]
[b]2. We may accidentally lynch a town killing role, and in this game they can't roleclaim against it.[/b]
3. I strongly believe a lynch in one week won't be good. Reading so many messages a day with so many people and extremely complex relationships is difficult and almost unmanageable. In fact, some people haven't even confirmed yet! Scum can more easily get a lynch on someone who is actually good and given there are so many people, bandwagoning is easier and harder to track.[b] tl;dr it's easier for scum to get away with killing a good townie then other games. [/b]

[/hide]
DISCLAIMER: I am not against scumhunting. Have your suspicions all you want; they will help later on and can have impacts later on in the game. Ways this can happen is: 1. You influence a town killing role to actually kill scum/3rd. 2. You influence votes later.
So scumhunting can continue![/quote]
It's good that you're not against scumhunting. Now do some yourself
No-lynch means scum controls more kills than otherwise, so we need to lynch at this point. Period.

t4s wrote:
Yes, a lynch on D1 is useful in most other mafia games. Agreed. But let's look at this game again and understand the differences from other games that have been played.
1. There are 32 people and closed setup.
2. It's one week long. [Just had to put it out there]
3. Hard roleclaiming can get you killed.

So this game is very different from basically every other MG in aops. And so the theory can vary a little. I believe one of these is whether we lynch day 1.

First we must first examine the arguments that support a D1 lynch.
1. It allows for a MYLO instead of LYLO if we need it.
2. It gives town the power to kill who they want.
3. It get rids of a worse townie, making town better off, even in the worst case.


So why do these reasons not work here?
1. This isn't a Newbie game, we don't know how many die per week and more.
2. We may accidentally lynch a town killing role, and in this game they can't roleclaim against it.
3. I strongly believe a lynch in one week won't be good. Reading so many messages a day with so many people and extremely complex relationships is difficult and almost unmanageable. In fact, some people haven't even confirmed yet! Scum can more easily get a lynch on someone who is actually good and given there are so many people, bandwagoning is easier and harder to track. tl;dr it's easier for scum to get away with killing a good townie then other games.

None of this contradicts the lylo logic of abcdefg....

Yes, a lynch on D1 is useful in most other mafia games. Agreed. But let's look at this game again and understand the differences from other games that have been played.
1. There are 32 people and closed setup.
2. It's one week long. [Just had to put it out there]
3. Hard roleclaiming can get you killed.

So this game is very different from basically every other MG in aops. And so the theory can vary a little. I believe one of these is whether we lynch day 1.

First we must first examine the arguments that support a D1 lynch.
1. It allows for a MYLO instead of LYLO if we need it.
2. It gives town the power to kill who they want.
3. It get rids of a worse townie, making town better off, even in the worst case.


So why do these reasons not work here?
1. This isn't a Newbie game, we don't know how many die per week and more.
2. We may accidentally lynch a town killing role, and in this game they can't roleclaim against it.
3. I strongly believe a lynch in one week won't be good. Reading so many messages a day with so many people and extremely complex relationships is difficult and almost unmanageable. In fact, some people haven't even confirmed yet! Scum can more easily get a lynch on someone who is actually good and given there are so many people, bandwagoning is easier and harder to track. tl;dr it's easier for scum to get away with killing a good townie then other games.

First we must first examine the arguments that support a D1 lynch.
1. It allows for a MYLO instead of LYLO if we need it. 
2. It gives town the power to kill who they want.
3. It get rids of a worse townie, making town better off, even in the worst case.

darn 8char

what is this.

Also,
FoS: atomicpianowitch
Reason: Remember on day 1 apw got bigmath's role. He thus knew that apw could easily win with either side, and thus did not need to take a side. After this, apw said that his role was 3rd and he could win with town. Fair, but this caused people to target bigmath, forcing him to roleclaim, and thus get MKed.
In other words, apw's revelation of bigmath's role caused his MK. I claim that this move is scummy.
After knowing bigmath's role, apw knows bigmath can win together with town or scum. He knows that if he doesn't reveal bigmath's alignment no one will know what the prize is. (Since only he got it). So he has the choice of not claiming it. Also note that bigmath was only in moderate attacks at that time, not too severe to get lynched D1 but possibly lynched later anyways.

Assume apw is town. Then he should realise that bigmath... well... doesn't really matter. Showing his alignment doesn't affect scumhunting and saying "third-party" would likely raise doubts about bigmath's role, possibly leading to his lynch. apw could even have lied to clear bigmath so that he would not be lynched but instead targetted by others to die. So this isn't the best way for him to play as town.

Assume apw is scum. A claim of someone's alignment being third party resulting in a lynch on him is seemingly innocent and simple to get away from. I mean, all apw did was to reveal his alignment; who would have known this lead to his lynch, or even, his MK? Being scum, wanting to lynch non-scum would be his goal, and this easily gets him there. This is a good play for scum.

Thus, I think that apw's move back there says something.

SFGF wrote:
You just want the game to slide into night without any information, and then D2 ends up being just as informed as D1

And I know you know why no lynching is bad -_-
1. We do not go with no information; we have the night kills.
2. I have explained why no lynch shouldn't be done, and I guess I'll have to do it again.
3. NL is bad on Newbie games and C9 and the rest because of the parity, and partially also because people can roleclaim without dying..


Now I'm going to give my case for No Lynch today. Again. Because people are dismissing it since they say "this is just another MG, we should also lynch". That's being really lazy and not bothering to understand this game well.
My case for a no lynch
Yes, a lynch on D1 is useful in most other mafia games. Agreed. But let's look at this game again and understand the differences from other games that have been played.
1. There are 32 people and closed setup.
2. It's one week long. [Just had to put it out there]
3. Hard roleclaiming can get you killed.

So this game is very different from basically every other MG in aops. And so the theory can vary a little. I believe one of these is whether we lynch day 1.

First we must first examine the arguments that support a D1 lynch.
1. It allows for a MYLO instead of LYLO if we need it.
2. It gives town the power to kill who they want.
3. It get rids of a worse townie, making town better off, even in the worst case.


So why do these reasons not work here?
1. This isn't a Newbie game, we don't know how many die per week and more.
2. We may accidentally lynch a town killing role, and in this game they can't roleclaim against it.
3. I strongly believe a lynch in one week won't be good. Reading so many messages a day with so many people and extremely complex relationships is difficult and almost unmanageable. In fact, some people haven't even confirmed yet! Scum can more easily get a lynch on someone who is actually good and given there are so many people, bandwagoning is easier and harder to track. tl;dr it's easier for scum to get away with killing a good townie then other games.

DISCLAIMER: I am not against scumhunting. Have your suspicions all you want; they will help later on and can have impacts later on in the game. Ways this can happen is: 1. You influence a town killing role to actually kill scum/3rd. 2. You influence votes later.
So scumhunting can continue!

1. We do not go with no information; we have the night kills.
2. I have explained why no lynch shouldn't be done, and I guess I'll have to do it again.
3. NL is bad on Newbie games and C9 and the rest because of the parity, and partially also because [b]people can roleclaim without dying.[/b].
[/hide]

Now I'm going to give my case for No Lynch today. Again. Because people are dismissing it since they say "this is just another MG, we should also lynch". That's being really lazy and not bothering to understand this game well. 
[hide=My case for a no lynch]
Yes, a lynch on D1 is useful in most other mafia games. Agreed. But let's look at this game again and understand the differences from other games that have been played.
1. There are 32 people and closed setup.
2. It's one week long. [Just had to put it out there]
3. [b]Hard roleclaiming can get you killed.[/b]

So this game is very different from basically every other MG in aops. And so the theory can vary a little. I believe one of these is whether we lynch day 1. 

First we must first examine the arguments that support a D1 lynch.
[b]1. It allows for a MYLO instead of LYLO if we need it. [/b]
[b]2. It gives town the power to kill who they want.[/b]
[b]3. It get rids of a worse townie, making town better off, even in the worst case.[/b]


So why do these reasons not work here?
[b]1. This isn't a Newbie game, we don't know how many die per week and more.[/b]
[b]2. We may accidentally lynch a town killing role, and in this game they can't roleclaim against it.[/b]
3. I strongly believe a lynch in one week won't be good. Reading so many messages a day with so many people and extremely complex relationships is difficult and almost unmanageable. In fact, some people haven't even confirmed yet! Scum can more easily get a lynch on someone who is actually good and given there are so many people, bandwagoning is easier and harder to track.[b] tl;dr it's easier for scum to get away with killing a good townie then other games. [/b]

[/hide]
DISCLAIMER: I am not against scumhunting. Have your suspicions all you want; they will help later on and can have impacts later on in the game. Ways this can happen is: 1. You influence a town killing role to actually kill scum/3rd. 2. You influence votes later.
So scumhunting can continue![/quote]

Walrus!
Event 3 is a walrus. This one's a little easier to explain. There are 3 categories, and each of the players will submit a song for each of the three categories which satisfies the requirements for the category. These are the categories:

1. Songs from Disney Musicals.
This one is pretty self explanatory. Disney has unlimited power and has made some pretty famous stuff, so this one should be easy to find.

2. Songs from My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic.
This one will be fun. I may be torturing some of you for my amusement, but you signed up, so I don't care. There is a lot of music in this show, both really good and really bad (I have certain songs in mind when I say this). You may also submit songs from the spinoff Equestria Girls films which are kinda dumb (but there is a lot of music).

3. Songs from the (18)30s and 40s.
It's TBT, so we are going wayyyyy back! This was before most of you were born, so you may not remember music from this time period very well. I'm not gonna strictly enforce the release date, but make an effort to pick music from those 2 decades.

I will rank each of the 9 songs from each of the 3 categories based on how much I like them, since how well you fit the category is a yes/no question for each of these. Submit your songs (please include links) in your Role PMs. You have until 7:00 AM PT on 10/8 to submit. Good Luck!
